I haven't played many racing/driving games, as far as I'm concerned this is a great game, I'm having lots of fun with it. They recently published an update featuring an offline mode so that the game will still be playable when the servers shut down which is great news
The only issue I can see is that it takes quite a long time to load the game

The Crew 2 is honestly really good. The map is massive and there’s always something to do, just driving or flying around is fun on its own. With the whole server situation no longer being an issue, it feels way better knowing the game will stick around.
Great game with good graphics but multiplayer is almost dead. You will not be able to find races with real players. And you can roam on New York Times Square.

Best all around vehicular racing game. No other like it lets you jump off a mountain with a agerra, instantly switch into a acrobatic plane, fly to the sea and drop into your racing boat, all without using menu.

My story of 1.8 hours of gameplay 1/10
- download game bc I wanna relax and drive sometimes
- boot up game one day when I wanna drive
- wait 5 minutes to start
- game won't start
-  check task manager
- click end task on launcher and game
- restart game
- wait another 5 minutes
- repeats a couple of times
- diced to play a different game
- play different game
- after 1.5 hours quit game
- see game has started up
- click on game
- screen constantly rearranges resolution and can't pick a good setting
- finally got to do something
- try to go to settings
- first have to watch intro
- skip intro
- cutscene
- skip cutscene
- un skippable cutscene
- finally done and go to settings
- resolution was half of my screen and on full screen
- set to window to move to second screen
- set setting to borderless on second screen
- game window moves to screen one
- tries to find setting to switch screen
- setting does not exist
- continues on screen 1
- grabs controller ps4
- plug ps4 controller in and try to play
- press every button possible
- game not supporting ps4 controllers
- quits game
- writes this review

It has... A lot of issues.
+Some premises are good, like the idea to be able to even race with planes.
+Also there's bikes. There's sports bikes, street bikes, custom bikes, offroad bikes. Not the most wide selection but there is at least something for everyone, which is half the reason I got this game in the first place.
+Though I haven't tried it, there's offline mode now, which I've seen people complain about there not being.
+You can actually travel from city to city, and some side quests require you to, I thought that was cool

But other than that...
-Maneuverability is... Mid. It's not Need for Speed levels of bad, but it's not Colin McRae levels of good. It's just there.
-Mileage makes absolutely no sense at all. Is mileage inflation a thing? No vehicle should be able to make a slow, precise turn at 100 km/h, and at 220+ km/h the slightest jump should be making you fly. In short, speeds don't feel like what the game shows you, at all.
-AI is pretty bad, and awkward.
-Side quests tend to be long and boring, or short, and boring.
-DEI annoying characters are all over the place, which has become kind of a standard for racing games as of late, it seems.
-Online is pretty much dead.
-Ubisoft launcher.
-BattlEye kernel bloatware.
-Tried to use borderless screen option and that forced the game to the right corner of my screen, making it unplayable. Managed to turn it back to fullscreen, only to see that my car had disappeared? Now the game was actually running, but there was no car on screen, and I could not play.

If you're just getting it to play with friends, this is a maybe on big sales, emphasis on maybe. And on big. And on sales.

The Crew 2 is the kind of game you do not play for realism — you play it because driving across America, switching from car to plane to boat, and chasing pure arcade freedom just feels good. It is messy, sometimes repetitive, and not always deep, but the vibe is unmatched. Few games let you just cruise, grind, race, collect, and relax like this one. After hundreds of hours, it became less of a racing game and more of a comfort game.

Verdict: Recommended for arcade racing fans.

Good luck racing in this game....not a racing game. I played it for 7 hours and have yet to purchase a car to upgrade. This game switches modes more than a Trani, good luck playing it, finding out the race, and having the right car. Big F to this game because of the cars.
It is fun because it's pure racing, free roam, and in ways tycoon like. You race, drive, and do stunts to earn money and "followers" to get faster, bigger, and better cars.

Terrible. The map is large sure, but it has no soul and it's basically mostly just quantity over quality. It's basically Forza Horizon excluding the extensive customisation, realistic yet enjoyable handling, and most importantly, passion. This game tried to achieve too much and ended up being good at none of them. I've said what I wanted to say about the map already, but the handling of the cars themselves are absolutely terrible and feels very floaty. That's not even mentioning about the other types of vehicles, which are less focused on and are even worse to deal with. 

As for the vehicles themselves, aside from the handling, they aren't very well made either. They look decent visually for a game from 2018, but what grinds my gears the most is the abhorrent upgrade system. It has no thought or passion put into it, just a bunch of codes and colour grading, a bit like NFS Payback but even more basic. The exterior customisation is nothing special either, mostly consisting of custom made splitters and such with absolutely zero branded custom rim brands.

The progression is ok at first but eventually it just becomes a mindless grind without any fun factor. Most of them don't even have anything that really incentivises you to start playing them, the race layouts are ok but of course, since the handling itself is inherently flawed, most would not be able to get themselves to play through it. There are some "story branches" to follow but even they are just filler slop, again, with no passion or thought put into it. All in all it's just a mindless grind that not only feels like a chore or a checklist to complete but is also outright not fun at all.

Overall, I do not see how this game caters to any audience. To those looking for arcade racers? Go with NFS or other racing games with more passion put towards it. Simcades? Go with Forza or Gran Turismo. Games with vehicles that aren't cars? Go with like Microsoft Flight Sim, Ace Combat, or even Grand Theft Auto. I rest my case.

The crew 2 is an open world racing game.

There is loads of vehicles to buy and upgrade.

You can form a crew of 4 players to hang out or make a races.

The new Hybrid mode allows players to play the game without an internet connection.

The only issue is that it takes about 5 minutes to load into the game.

My experience has been great so far and with the release of motorfest, its time to give the crew 2 a good earned rest.

I do recommend this game and allways will do.

I discovered this gem in 2026 due to boredom from forza horizon series. I got to say this game is the best racing game since Test Drive Unlimited 2 (2011). I played so many racing games since then but never felt  this much fun just like in TDU2. In Forza Horizon 4 or 5 or even the newest one you can drive super cars in about an hour after you start the game. But in this game progression is very well done. There is always something to do or grind if you want. The map is so big with so many cities to discover and animals to take photos and races and content its huge. Unbelievable game. I wish i could play this gem when its released. I will play until im done and then i will start on Crew Motorfest hopefully its as good as this game. I will never waste my hours on Forza again. In Forza cars might feel better to drive and graphics might be better but its not fun. Its the same thing over and over and you can do any race with any car its stupid. Im Crew 2 you got to upgrade your drifting car your drag car your motorcross vehicle. Its awesome. You might feel overwhelmed at first but after you upfrade your car a little bit and start to win races the game opens up. The amount of content in this game is top noth and even you can play in offline mode. So after few years if servers are shut down we will still be playing this gem. Highly recommend anyone.

This game is my ultimate comfort zone.

The customization in this game is amazing. And I spent hours just tweaking them and changing how they look until they were exactly how I wanted.

Now, I’ve completed 100% of the achievements and done everything there is to do. But even with nothing left to unlock, I still find myself opening the game all the time. Whenever I need to destress or just want to relax, I jump in for a joyride across the massive map just to look at the views.

It’s the perfect game to just turn your brain off and drive.

Overall Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (8/10)

Pros
Huge open-world map based on the United States.
Seamless switching between cars, boats, and airplanes.
Large variety of vehicles and racing events.
Fun arcade-style driving experience.
Plenty of content and updates added over the years.
Cons
Vehicle handling is not as refined as some competitors.
AI opponents can feel unfair at times.
Graphics and physics show their age compared to newer racing games.
Less realistic than simulation-focused racing titles.
Verdict

The Crew 2 is an enjoyable open-world racing game that offers freedom, exploration, and a wide selection of vehicles. While it may not match the driving quality of games like Forza Horizon 6, it remains a great choice for players who enjoy casual racing and exploring a massive map.

Final Score: 8/10 – Recommended for fans of arcade racing and open-world exploration.

# Review - The Crew 2 😤

---

**Title:** Great Concept, Ruined by Cheaters — Not Recommended in Its Current State

---

The Crew 2 had everything going for it — an open-world recreation of the entire United States, an insane variety of vehicles spanning cars, motorcycles, boats, and planes, and a progression system that kept you hooked for hours. On paper, it's a dream game.

In reality? It's a nightmare.

**Cheaters Everywhere — Every Single Type**

The moment you jump into any race or time trial, you're immediately greeted by waves of cheaters in every form imaginable:

- 🚀 **Speed Hackers** — They cross the finish line before you even leave the starting point
- 🔁 **Teleporters** — Disappear and reappear instantly at the front of the pack
- 💥 **God Mode Users** — Completely immune to crashes, collisions, and obstacles
- 📈 **Stat Manipulators** — Leaderboard numbers that defy physics and basic logic
- 🤖 **Bots & Fake Accounts** — Flooding sessions and making any real competition pointless

**Where is Ubisoft?**

This is perhaps the most frustrating part. You submit reports, and they vanish into thin air. There is no meaningful anti-cheat system in place, no visible action being taken, and no communication from the developers reassuring the community that this is being addressed. Honest players are left to suffer while cheaters run completely unchecked.

**Bottom Line**

If you're looking for a solo exploration experience, you might still squeeze some enjoyment out of it. But if you're here for competitive racing or any form of multiplayer, save yourself the frustration.

This game deserves better. The players who actually love it deserve better. Until Ubisoft takes cheating seriously, this is impossible to recommend.

**👎 Not Recommended** — Not because of the game itself, but because the cheating problem has completely destroyed any reason to play it .

[h3]Like Riders Republic, but for cars![/h3]
Fun factor, variety, and accessibility take the fore: wanna teleport to a street race in San Fran' from your boat time-trial in the NYC harbour? Go for it. (No need to drive ten minutes for a 90 second race here!)  However if you're looking to do 20+ minute coast-to-coast treks, they have those too!

Hit a sick jump and then swap from a dirtbike into a biplane midair at the press of a button, all in a beautiful world filled with wildlife and (unlike any other Crew or Forza) pedestrians on the streets like the good old days! And of course the glorious driver/internal cam'!

Bonus points that it's also one of the easiest games to set up a wheel for <3
ARD TO LOGIN BUT WHEN YOU DO IT IS FUN AS HECK
